root_causes: Frontend API base URL had localhost/port assumptions, breaking cross-machine execution.
Backend responses were inconsistent (mixed plain dicts, no success/error envelope), causing fragile frontend parsing.
Job lifecycle lacked explicit status/result endpoints and progress fields.
Frontend polling could hang without timeout/error state, leaving UI in stuck states.
CORS policy was wildcard-only and not environment-driven for multi-origin setup.
Machine-specific path exposure in responses made debugging and deployment harder.
No structured backend request logging for route/job tracing.
fixes_applied: Added environment-driven API base handling in frontend with runtime fallback and clear backend-offline indicators.
Introduced structured success/error JSON response patterns.
Added robust job lifecycle endpoints: /jobs/{job_id}/status and /jobs/{job_id}/result.
Added polling timeout and failure handling in frontend state management.
Implemented constrained CORS with configurable CORS_ORIGINS.
Added request middleware logging with request_id and duration to logs/backend.log.
Added Docker + docker-compose for reproducible multi-machine setup.
remaining_considerations: For production, move long-running jobs to task queue (Celery/RQ) and persist job state in Redis/DB.
For deployed frontend, inject FRONTEND_API_BASE_URL at build/runtime using platform env vars.
